questions.question: Prove that a metric space is compact if and only if every sequence in the space has a convergent subsequence. (8 hours)
Show that the metric space of real numbers equipped with the standard Euclidean metric is compact, but the metric space of real numbers equipped with the discrete metric is not compact. (8 hours)
Find a metric space that is not compact, but any sequence in the space has a convergent subsequence. (8 hours)
